groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Webinar subject, goal, audience, promise, teaching points, format, duration, CTA direction, language, brand, and presenter identity.

groups.title: Event Setup
groups.description: Scheduling, hosting platform, registration destination, and presenter details when these are known.

groups.title: Content & Engagement
groups.description: Source material, agenda preferences, audience knowledge, interaction choices, evidence, and topic-specific compliance requirements.

groups.title: Brand, Offer & Promotion
groups.description: Tone, brand voice, registration-page depth, offer positioning, proof, objections, and visual direction.

groups.title: Follow-Up & Output Settings
groups.description: CTA destination, follow-up objective, replay access, post-webinar resources, sender identity, and desired script depth.

variables.label: Webinar topic
variables.placeholder: Example: How small agencies can automate client reporting
variables.hint: Enter the main subject, product, service, or transformation the webinar will cover.

help.what: Provide the webinar topic for this webinar package.
help.why: The complete webinar package must be built around a clearly defined subject.
help.example: Good example: How small agencies can automate client reporting
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Primary webinar goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the most important result the webinar should support.

help.what: Provide the primary webinar goal for this webinar package.
help.why: The goal determines the webinar structure, CTA emphasis, and registration-page positioning.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Describe who should attend, including role, experience, industry, or situation.
variables.hint: Focus on the people the webinar is specifically designed to help.

help.what: Provide the target audience for this webinar package.
help.why: Audience context controls terminology, examples, depth, objections, and promotional messaging.
help.example: Good example: Small agency owners and account managers who manually prepare recurring client updates.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Audience problem
variables.placeholder: What problem, frustration, uncertainty, or missed opportunity brings them here?
variables.hint: Describe the current situation the webinar should help the audience improve.

help.what: Provide the audience problem for this webinar package.
help.why: The registration promise and teaching sequence need a specific audience problem.
help.example: Good example: They spend hours assembling reports and struggle to make results clear to clients.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Core webinar promise
variables.placeholder: What should attendees understand, achieve, or be able to do afterward?
variables.hint: Use a realistic outcome rather than a guaranteed result.

help.what: Provide the core webinar promise for this webinar package.
help.why: The promise anchors the title, registration copy, opening, agenda, and closing.
help.example: Good example: Attendees will understand a practical workflow for clearer, faster client reporting.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Key takeaways
variables.placeholder: List the main lessons, methods, decisions, or steps attendees should receive.
variables.hint: Three to six concrete takeaways usually work well.

help.what: Provide the key takeaways for this webinar package.
help.why: The content plan cannot be accurately created without the intended learning outcomes.
help.example: Good example: Map recurring reporting questions, choose the right metrics, build a repeatable agenda, and prepare a follow-up plan.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Webinar format
variables.placeholder: Select a format
variables.hint: This controls scheduling language, interaction, replay wording, and presenter notes.

help.what: Provide the webinar format for this webinar package.
help.why: Live, evergreen, and premiered webinars require different presentation and registration language.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Planned duration
variables.hint: Enter the total planned duration in minutes, including the CTA and Q&A.

help.what: Optional context for the planned duration field.
help.why: Duration determines the number of segments, pacing, slides, examples, and interaction moments.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Primary CTA
variables.placeholder: Example: Book a consultation or start a free trial
variables.hint: Use Auto to let the model choose a suitable next step from the supplied goal.

help.what: Optional context for the primary cta field.
help.why: A webinar should lead toward one clear and proportionate next action.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, German, or Spanish
variables.hint: All public-facing webinar content will be created in this language.

help.what: Provide the output language for this webinar package.
help.why: The output language cannot always be safely inferred from the topic.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported promises, guaranteed outcomes, fake urgency, or changing the webinar into a different topic.

variables.label: Brand or company
variables.placeholder: Enter the organization, product, or brand name
variables.hint: Leave blank when the webinar is presented independently.

help.what: Optional context for the brand or company field.
help.why: The brand name may be required in registration copy, introductions, CTAs, and emails.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Presenter name
variables.placeholder: Enter the host or main presenter name
variables.hint: Leave blank to use neutral presenter references without inventing a name.

help.what: Optional context for the presenter name field.
help.why: A supplied presenter name allows accurate introductions, biographies, and email sign-offs.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Event date
variables.hint: Used only for scheduled live events or recorded premieres.

help.what: Optional context for the event date field.
help.why: Registration copy needs the actual date when the event occurs at a scheduled time.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Event time
variables.placeholder: Example: 3:00 PM
variables.hint: Enter the scheduled start time in the format your audience expects.

help.what: Optional context for the event time field.
help.why: Scheduled-event copy must display the correct time without inventing it.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Time zone
variables.placeholder: Example: EET, UTC+3, or America/New_York
variables.hint: Include a time zone whenever attendees may be in different locations.

help.what: Optional context for the time zone field.
help.why: Time-zone clarity prevents misleading or ambiguous event scheduling.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Registration URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com/webinar
variables.hint: Used in registration, confirmation, reminder, or promotional references when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the registration url field.
help.why: A real destination can be included without fabricating a registration link.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, unapproved, or misleading URLs.

variables.label: Hosting platform
variables.placeholder: Example: Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, YouTube, or a custom platform
variables.hint: Used only when platform-specific participation instructions are needed.

help.what: Optional context for the hosting platform field.
help.why: The hosting environment may affect joining instructions, interaction options, and terminology.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Presenter role
variables.placeholder: Example: Product strategist, founder, researcher, or customer success lead
variables.hint: Describe the presenter’s relevant role without adding unsupported credentials.

help.what: Optional context for the presenter role field.
help.why: The presenter’s actual role helps establish relevant context and authority.
help.example: Good example: Founder and content workflow strategist
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Presenter bio and credentials
variables.placeholder: Add approved experience, qualifications, achievements, and relevant background.
variables.hint: Only include credentials and claims that can be publicly used.

help.what: Optional context for the presenter bio and credentials field.
help.why: Registration pages often need a factual presenter section, but credentials must not be invented.
help.example: Good example: Founder building workflow tools for creators and small teams; use only approved credentials.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Additional presenters
variables.placeholder: List names, roles, and speaking responsibilities for any additional presenters.
variables.hint: Leave blank for a single-presenter webinar.

help.what: Optional context for the additional presenters field.
help.why: Multiple presenters require accurate introductions and clear segment ownership.
help.example: Good example: Jordan Lee — moderator and Q&A support.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Source material
variables.placeholder: Paste approved facts, notes, research, product details, links, or existing content.
variables.hint: Use this for information the model must rely on rather than infer.

help.what: Optional context for the source material field.
help.why: Source material improves factual accuracy and prevents unsupported content.
help.example: Good example: Use only supplied product notes, agenda notes, screenshots, and approved customer-safe examples.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Agenda preferences
variables.placeholder: List required sections, demonstrations, exercises, stories, or discussion topics.
variables.hint: Leave blank to let the model build the agenda from the promised takeaways.

help.what: Optional context for the agenda preferences field.
help.why: Some webinars have required segments that cannot be safely inferred.
help.example: Good example: Opening, reporting problem, workflow framework, short demo, Q&A, and consultation CTA.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Audience knowledge level
variables.placeholder: Select the expected level
variables.hint: Controls terminology, explanations, examples, and assumed background knowledge.

help.what: Optional context for the audience knowledge level field.
help.why: The same subject requires substantially different explanations at different knowledge levels.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Interaction methods
variables.placeholder: Choose interaction formats; or list supported methods separated by commas.
variables.hint: Select only methods supported by the planned webinar environment. Available options: Auto, Live chat, Polls, Q&A, Short exercises, Breakout discussion, Knowledge quiz, No interaction.

help.what: Optional context for the interaction methods field.
help.why: Interaction choices affect pacing, presenter transitions, and the run of show.
help.example: Good example: Live chat, Q&A, short exercises
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Likely audience questions
variables.placeholder: Add common questions, concerns, misunderstandings, or objections.
variables.hint: Used to prepare Q&A prompts and strengthen the teaching sequence.

help.what: Optional context for the likely audience questions field.
help.why: Known audience questions help make the webinar more useful and relevant.
help.example: Good example: How much reporting detail is enough? What should be automated first?
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Evidence and examples
variables.placeholder: Add approved data, examples, demonstrations, case details, or quotations.
variables.hint: Unsupported statistics, testimonials, and results will not be invented.

help.what: Optional context for the evidence and examples field.
help.why: Specific proof and examples must come from supplied, approved information.
help.example: Good example: Use the approved example of replacing a manual weekly update with a reusable report outline.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Topic sensitivity
variables.placeholder: Select when special care is required
variables.hint: This activates cautious wording and relevant verification reminders.

help.what: Optional context for the topic sensitivity field.
help.why: High-stakes subjects require stricter claim handling and appropriate limitations.
help.avoid: Avoid personalized medical, legal, financial, employment, political, child-safety, or regulated advice unless supplied review requirements support safe general wording.

variables.label: Compliance requirements
variables.placeholder: Add jurisdiction, approved disclaimer, prohibited claims, or review requirements.
variables.hint: Provide only requirements that actually apply to this webinar.

help.what: Optional context for the compliance requirements field.
help.why: Regulated content may require jurisdiction-specific language that the model cannot safely infer.
help.example: Good example: Avoid guaranteeing revenue, savings, or client retention improvements.
help.avoid: Avoid personalized medical, legal, financial, employment, political, child-safety, or regulated advice unless supplied review requirements support safe general wording.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred tone
variables.hint: The model will keep the tone appropriate for both speaking and registration copy.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially changes the registration copy and presenter delivery style.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Describe preferred vocabulary, personality, rhythm, and phrases to avoid.
variables.hint: Leave blank for a natural, professional, non-corporate voice.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: Established brands may require a voice that cannot be inferred from the topic alone.
help.example: Good example: Helpful, precise, low-pressure, practical.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Registration page length
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred depth
variables.hint: Controls the depth of the public registration-page copy.

help.what: Optional context for the registration page length field.
help.why: A simple event page and a high-consideration webinar need different copy depth.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Webinar offer type
variables.placeholder: Select what follows the webinar
variables.hint: Choose the offer or next step presented during the CTA section.

help.what: Optional context for the webinar offer type field.
help.why: The CTA structure depends on whether the webinar teaches only or introduces an offer.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Describe the offer, price, inclusions, eligibility, availability, and approved terms.
variables.hint: Only supplied pricing, deadlines, bonuses, and results may be mentioned.

help.what: Optional context for the offer details field.
help.why: Accurate CTA copy requires the actual offer details and limitations.
help.example: Good example: Free 20-minute workflow consultation; no guaranteed outcomes; booking URL supplied separately.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Approved social proof
variables.placeholder: Add verified testimonials, results, client names, ratings, or adoption data.
variables.hint: Leave blank rather than asking the model to create proof.

help.what: Optional context for the approved social proof field.
help.why: Social proof is useful only when genuine, approved evidence has been supplied.
help.example: Good example: Only use verified testimonials or results supplied here; otherwise omit proof.
help.avoid: Avoid invented facts, credentials, testimonials, results, prices, deadlines, examples, links, offers, or presenter details that were not supplied.

variables.label: Objections to address
variables.placeholder: List concerns that may prevent registration, participation, trust, or action.
variables.hint: The model will answer objections without using pressure or fear.

help.what: Optional context for the objections to address field.
help.why: Known objections influence registration copy, teaching emphasis, and CTA framing.
help.example: Good example: Attendees may worry automation will make reports generic or too technical.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Visual direction
variables.placeholder: Describe preferred slide style, brand visuals, diagrams, demos, or imagery.
variables.hint: Used for presentation guidance, not for inventing unsupported visual evidence.

help.what: Optional context for the visual direction field.
help.why: Webinars benefit from presentation-specific visual planning when preferences exist.
help.example: Good example: Clean slides, simple workflow diagrams, and screenshots only when supplied.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: CTA destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com/next-step
variables.hint: Leave blank to describe the action without inventing a destination.

help.what: Optional context for the cta destination url field.
help.why: The webinar and follow-up email may need a real destination for the primary CTA.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, unapproved, or misleading URLs.

variables.label: Follow-up goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main follow-up objective
variables.hint: Controls the focus of the post-webinar email.

help.what: Optional context for the follow-up goal field.
help.why: The follow-up message should support one clear next step rather than several competing goals.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Follow-up timing
variables.placeholder: Select the recommended send time
variables.hint: Used in the delivery recommendation, not as a fabricated scheduled event.

help.what: Optional context for the follow-up timing field.
help.why: Timing affects the framing and urgency of the follow-up email.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Replay availability
variables.placeholder: Select the replay policy
variables.hint: Choose an actual replay window or let the model avoid making a specific claim.

help.what: Optional context for the replay availability field.
help.why: Replay wording must not invent access, scarcity, or expiration dates.
help.avoid: Avoid claiming replay access, expiration, scarcity, or ongoing availability unless it is confirmed.

variables.label: Post-webinar resource
variables.placeholder: Describe any checklist, workbook, slides, template, recording, or bonus resource.
variables.hint: Only resources that actually exist will be included.

help.what: Optional context for the post-webinar resource field.
help.why: A supplied resource may shape both the closing and the follow-up email.
help.example: Good example: Reporting checklist PDF, if supplied.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Follow-up sender name
variables.placeholder: Enter the person or brand sending the email
variables.hint: Falls back to the presenter or brand name when available.

help.what: Optional context for the follow-up sender name field.
help.why: A real sender identity improves clarity and prevents invented attribution.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Presenter content depth
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred script depth
variables.hint: Controls whether the presentation section contains an outline, notes, or fuller narration.

help.what: Optional context for the presenter content depth field.
help.why: Different presenters need substantially different levels of scripting support.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining requirements, restrictions, terminology, or background.
variables.hint: Use this only for information not already covered by another field.

help.what: Optional context for the additional context field.
help.why: A single controlled field handles relevant edge cases without expanding the form unnecessarily.
help.example: Good example: No fake urgency, fake scarcity, invented attendee counts, or unsupported product claims.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake scarcity, fake attendee counts, hidden promotion, manipulative pressure, or unsupported claims.
